Fujianese organized crime.description: Fujianese organized crime refers to crime syndicates composed of Fujianese people, similar to triad gangs. The term primarily describes Fujianese immigrant gangs in Hong Kong but also includes native crime syndicates in Fujian province. They have been involved in attacks against Hong Kong pro-democracy protesters and collaborate with various criminal organizations and pro-Beijing groups.

descriptions: Big Circle Gang is allied with Fujian gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Fujian gangs

descriptions: Fuqing Gang collaborated with American Mafia in underground casinos and drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Hong Kong media reports indicated that the Fuqing Gang collaborated with the Japanese Yakuza , the American Mafia , and Triads operating in mainland China in the operation of underground casinos and the trafficking of illegal drugs in East Asia.

descriptions: Fujianese organized crime is allied with American Mafia.

Chijon family.description: The Chijon family was a South Korean gang active from 1993 to 1994, founded by Kim Gi-hwan and six others. The gang targeted wealthy individuals, committing murder, extortion, kidnapping, cannibalism, and rape. It was dismantled after a series of arrests and trials.

descriptions: Maggapa was a copycat gang inspired by the Chijon Family.
edges.evidence_quote: At the same time as the 1996 North Korean commandos infiltration incident , there was a South Korean nine-member copycat gang inspired by the Chijon Family. The copycat gang called themselves the Maggapa (막가파).

descriptions: Kurdistan Communities Union fought against the Islamic State.

descriptions: Kurdistan Communities Union is listed as an additional opponent of Islamic State in the infobox.

descriptions: PKK members founded KCK as an umbrella organization.
edges.evidence_quote: In May 2007, serving and former members of the PKK set up the Kurdistan Communities Union (KCK), an umbrella organisation of Kurdish organisations in Turkish, Iraqi, Iranian, and Syrian Kurdistan.

descriptions: PKK has been part of the KCK since 2005.
edges.evidence_quote: Allegiance: Kurdistan Communities Union (since 2005)

descriptions: The ABT fought wars with the Texas Mafia in 1995/1996, resulting in the TM being 'parked'.
edges.evidence_quote: Texas Mafia (TM) was 'parked' system wide, meaning they were no longer allowed to recruit members, after the ABT–TM wars in 1995/1996.

Wah Kee.description: Wah Kee is a secret society based in Malaysia and Singapore since the 19th century, founded in Singapore by Sū Guǎnghuá. It operates in multiple Malaysian states and Singapore's Chinatown, with branches in Australia, Thailand, Vietnam, China, Hong Kong, the United States, Canada, and New Zealand. The group is heavily involved in heroin trafficking in the region.

descriptions: Wah Kee has 08 as a rival according to infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: 24, 36, 08

descriptions: Wah Kee has 24 as a rival according to infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: 24, 36, 08

descriptions: Guang Ji was created by an associate of Wah Kee's founder, indicating a structural tie.
edges.evidence_quote: His associate Zhào Guǎnghéng (赵广恒) created Guǎng Jì (广记) in Guangzhou , China

descriptions: Jie Ji was created by an associate of Wah Kee's founder, indicating a structural tie.

descriptions: Schultz sought permission from the Commission and later was ordered killed by them.
edges.evidence_quote: Schultz asked the Commission, the governing body of the American Mafia, for permission to kill Dewey... When Schultz disobeyed them and made an attempt to kill Dewey, the Commission ordered his murder in 1935.

descriptions: The American Mafia, through its Commission, was in conflict with Schultz's gang, culminating in Schultz's murder.
edges.evidence_quote: Schultz asked the Commission, the governing body of the American Mafia, for permission to kill Dewey, in an attempt to avert his conviction. They refused. When Schultz disobeyed them and made an attempt to kill Dewey, the Commission ordered his murder in 1935.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Five Families as the allegiance of the Noe-Schultz Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allegiance: New York City's Five Families

descriptions: John M. Dunn, a member of the Hell's Kitchen Irish mob, was an associate of Schultz's gang and was not targeted in the assassination.
edges.evidence_quote: No apparent attempt was made on the life of Irish mobster John M. Dunn, who later became the brother-in-law of mobster Edward J. McGrath and a powerful member of the Hell's Kitchen Irish mob.

descriptions: The Noe-Schultz gang fought a bootleg war against the Irish mob led by Legs Diamond.
edges.evidence_quote: this brazen move led to a bootleg war with New York's Irish mob, led by Jack 'Legs' Diamond.

descriptions: Schultz is believed to have ordered the murder of Jewish mob boss Arnold Rothstein.
edges.evidence_quote: Although George 'Hump' McManus supposedly killed Rothstein over an unpaid gambling debt, Schultz is believed to have ordered the killing in retribution for Noe's death.

descriptions: Murder, Inc. carried out the Commission's order to kill Dutch Schultz.
edges.evidence_quote: the Commission ordered Louis Buchalter to eliminate 'The Dutchman.'

descriptions: Schultz proposed a murder to the National Crime Syndicate, which later voted against it.
edges.evidence_quote: Schultz had proposed to the National Crime Syndicate, a confederation of mobsters, that Dewey be murdered.

descriptions: The Noe-Schultz Gang was aligned with the Five Families.
edges.evidence_quote: Allegiance: New York City's Five Families

descriptions: Schultz's gang sought permission from the Commission to kill Dewey; the Commission refused and later ordered Schultz's murder after he disobeyed.
edges.evidence_quote: Schultz asked the Commission, the governing body of the American Mafia, for permission to kill Dewey, in an attempt to avert his conviction. They refused. When Schultz disobeyed them and made an attempt to kill Dewey, the Commission ordered his murder in 1935.

descriptions: Morabito engaged in a feud with Palamara and Scriva in 1985.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 1985 scoppia la Faida di Motticella con i Palamara-Scriva.

descriptions: Morabito maintains strong ties with Scriva.
edges.evidence_quote: The Morabitos also maintain strong ties with the Maisano, Pansera, Versace, Zappia, Mollica, Criaco, and Scriva 'ndrine.

descriptions: Morabito has ties with Scriva.
edges.evidence_quote: Ma possono contare su legami con i Maisano, Pansera, Versace, Zappia, Mollica, Criaco e Scriva.

Kokusui-kai.description: The Kokusui-kai is a Tokyo-based yakuza organization founded in 1958 by Masaji Morita. It has an estimated 1000 members and is known for controlling Tokyo's fashionable Ginza district. Despite its relatively low membership, it is considered wealthy and successful. It was originally a member of the Kantō Hatsukakai but became an affiliate of the Yamaguchi-gumi in 2005.

descriptions: Kokusui-kai was a member of the Kantō Hatsukakai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang had long been a member of the Kantō Hatsukakai, a federation of Tokyo yakuza groups opposed to the powerful, Kansai -based Yamaguchi-gumi.

descriptions: Kokusui-kai and Sumiyoshi-kai were engaged in shootings and retaliations over territory.
edges.evidence_quote: The suicide followed a rash of shootings and retaliations between Kokusui-kai and a rival gang, Sumiyoshi-kai, over gang territory, including the shooting death of Sumiyoshi-kai's boss.

descriptions: Kokusui-kai became an affiliate of Yamaguchi-gumi after a sakazuki ceremony.
edges.evidence_quote: As a result of the ritual, the Kokusui-kai became an affiliate of their former enemies, the Yamaguchi-gumi.

descriptions: Kokusui-kai became an affiliate of Yamaguchi-gumi in August 2005.
edges.evidence_quote: This changed in August 2005, when in a surprise move, the Kokusui-kai withdrew from the Kantō alliance and became an affiliate of the Yamaguchi-gumi.

descriptions: Kokusui-kai and Yamaguchi-gumi agreed to stop fighting, indicating a truce.
edges.evidence_quote: The shootings ended with an agreement between the Kokusui-kai and Yamaguchi-gumi to stop fighting on 8 February.

descriptions: Yamaguchi-gumi absorbed Kokusui-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: They absorbed the Tokyo-based gang Kokusui-kai

descriptions: Euphrates Volcano is a founding member of SDF.
edges.evidence_quote: The following groups signed the founding document: - People's Protection Units (Yekîneyên Parastina Gel, YPG) - Women's Protection Units (Yekîneyên Parastina Jin, YPJ) - Al-Sanadid Forces - Syriac Military Council (Mawtbo Fulhoyo Suryoyo, MFS) - Liwa Thuwar al-Raqqa - Euphrates Volcano - Army of Revolutionaries (Jaysh al-Thuwar, JAT) 99th Infantry Brigade - Brigade Groups of al-Jazira

descriptions: SAC and Silent Aryan Warriors jointly planned a bomb plot against Jewish athletes.
edges.evidence_quote: members of the group, along with members of the Silent Aryan Warriors (another white supremacist prison gang), allegedly planned to attack Jewish athletes at the 2002 Winter Olympics

descriptions: SAC and Silent Aryan Warriors were charged together in a drug and firearms distribution case.
edges.evidence_quote: 21 members of the SAC, Silent Aryan Warriors, and Noble Elect Thugs were charged in Utah with distribution of drugs

descriptions: Clan Alfieri was in conflict with the Nuvoletta-Gionta cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: come quelli con il cartello Nuvoletta - Gionta

descriptions: Fukui-gumi is an affiliate of Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Irie began his career as a yakuza when he joined the Fukui-gumi, a Yamaguchi affiliate based in Osaka.

descriptions: Cozzolino clan is a rival of Vollaro clan per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mazzarella clan, Birra clan, Cozzolino clan

descriptions: The two sets were involved in a conflict that led to the 2022 Sacramento mass shooting.
edges.evidence_quote: the shooting was related to a conflict between the local Garden Blocc Crips and Del Paso Heights Bloods gangs

descriptions: Nutty Blocc Compton Crips are allies with Garden Blocc Crips.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: South Side Compton Crips , Atlantic Drive Compton Crips Garden Blocc Crips Sacramento Ca

descriptions: Lo Presti formed alliances with the Stalteri 'ndrina in Canada.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto rapporti di affari con la Famiglia Inzerillo -Spatola-Gambino di Palermo , attraverso la quale strinse alleanze anche oltreoceano con la Famiglia Gambino di New York e con le famiglie della 'Ndrangheta in Canada degli Zito , dei Commisso , dei Racco, degli Stalteri a Toronto e dei Cotroni a Montréal .

descriptions: Shamali Khilafat joined ISKP.
edges.evidence_quote: Notable among these are the Omar Ghazi Group, Shamali Khilafat, Gansu Hui Group, Harakat Khilafat Baluch, Jaysh ul Islam, Mullah Bakhtwar's group, the Shamsatoo group, and radical student groups in Pakistan and eastern Afghanistan.

Piserà 'ndrina.description: The Piserà 'ndrina is a clan of the 'Ndrangheta, a criminal organization based in Tropea, Calabria, Italy. Active since the 1980s, it is allied with the Mancuso 'ndrina and has been in a feud with the La Rosa 'ndrina since the 1990s. The clan has been involved in violence, including the killing of municipal councilor Pasquale Piserà in 1982, and members have been convicted in major trials.

descriptions: La Rosa is in a feud with the Piserà 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: The La Rosa are in feud with the rival clan the Piserà 'ndrina

descriptions: La Rosa is in a feud with Piserà.
edges.evidence_quote: sono in faida con il clan rivale dei Piserà

descriptions: Piserà 'ndrina is a sub-unit of 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: The Piserà 'ndrina are a clan of the 'Ndrangheta

descriptions: Piserà 'ndrina and Mancuso 'ndrina are allies.
edges.evidence_quote: they are allied with the Mancuso 'ndrina

descriptions: The Piserà clan is allied with the Mancuso clan.
edges.evidence_quote: I Piserà sono una 'ndrina di Tropea vicina ai Mancuso .

descriptions: Piserà 'ndrina and La Rosa 'ndrina are in a feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Since the 1990s, they are in a feud with the La Rosa 'ndrina

descriptions: The Piserà clan was in a feud with the La Rosa clan in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni '90 del secolo scorso erano in faida con i La Rosa , anch'essi di Tropea .

The Extraditables.description: Narcoterrorist organization created by Colombian drug lords in the early to mid 1980s to oppose extradition to the United States. Its members were largely drawn from the Medellín Cartel, with leaders including Pablo Escobar and Gonzalo Rodríguez Gacha. The group engaged in violent acts such as murder, bombing, and kidnapping to pressure the Colombian government. It was active from 1984 until 1993.

descriptions: The Extraditables were allies of Cali Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Extraditables (defunct)

descriptions: The Extraditables is listed as an ally of Cali Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Extraditables (defunct)

descriptions: The Extraditables is listed as an ally of Medellín Cartel in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Guadalajara Cartel (defunct) , The Extraditables (defunct) , La Corporación (defunct) , Oficina de Envigado , Los Priscos (defunct) , Gallón Gang (defunct) , Galician clans , Gulf Cartel , Nicaragua (alleged) , Cuba (alleged) , Panama (alleged, under Manuel Noriega )

descriptions: Medellín Cartel and The Extraditables were allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Extraditables (defunct)

descriptions: The Extraditables were allied with the Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Medellín Cartel and associates

descriptions: Racial Volunteer Force split from Combat 18 but retains close links.
edges.evidence_quote: A group calling itself the Racial Volunteer Force split from C18 in 2002, although it has retained close links to its parent organisation.

descriptions: Rocco Moretti ordered the killing of Giosuè Rizzi because Rizzi was approaching the rival Sinesi-Francavilla clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Secondo le rivelazioni di Domenico Milella, boss barese collaboratore di giustizia, Rizzi sarebbe stato ucciso su ordine di Rocco Moretti (detenuto) il quale non avrebbe gradito l’avvicinamento di Rizzi ai rivali del clan Sinesi-Francavilla.

descriptions: The two clans have been in violent armed conflict for internal leadership since 2013.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 2013 si è verificata una polarizzazione del potere criminale sulle prime due batterie, impegnate fra loro in una violenta contrapposizione armata per la leadership interna

descriptions: The two batteries are described as opposing clans, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Ventiquattro ore dopo sono fermati il 39enne Luigi Biscotti, nipote di Roberto Sinesi, boss del clan opposto, e il 29enne Ciro Spinelli, ritenuti gli autori materiali del tentato omicidio.

descriptions: A member of Sinesi-Francavilla implicates a member of Trisciuoglio-Prencipe-Tolonese in a murder, indicating a connection but not specifying cooperation or conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Secondo le dichiarazioni di Patrizio Villani, sicario garganico al soldo della batteria Sinesi-Francavilla e condannato a 30 anni di reclusione per l'omicidio Tizzano, l'autore dell'omicidio Panunzio fu in realtà Federico Trisciuoglio, boss dell'omonima batteria Trisciuoglio-Prencipe-Tolonese e non Donato Delli Carri.

descriptions: Delli Carri is a sub-unit (costola) of Sinesi-Francavilla.
edges.evidence_quote: Donato Delli Carri, 30 anni, nipote di Roberto Sinesi, affiliato alla batteria Delli Carri, costola del clan Sinesi-Francavilla

descriptions: Delli Carri is a sub-group (costola) of Sinesi-Francavilla.
edges.evidence_quote: Le indagini consentono di documentare l'operatività della batteria Delli Carri (costola dei Sinesi-Francavilla)

descriptions: Roberto Sinesi of the Sinesi-Francavilla clan is linked to the 'Ndrangheta 'ndrina of Franco Coco Trovato.
edges.evidence_quote: Roberto Sinesi, legato alla 'ndrina calabrese di Franco Coco Trovato (alleato dei De Stefano di Reggio Calabria).

descriptions: Sinesi-Francavilla is organically inserted into 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: i Sinesi-Francavilla, inseriti organicamente nella 'Ndrangheta calabrese

descriptions: The Moretti-Pellegrino battery was excluded from the alliance between Sinesi-Francavilla and Trisciuoglio-Prencipe.
edges.evidence_quote: Da questo accordo venne esclusa la batteria Moretti-Pellegrino, guidata dalle carceri da Rocco Moretti

descriptions: Retaliation for the attack on Lanza, indicating conflict between the two batteries.
edges.evidence_quote: Secondo le dichiarazioni del pentito Carlo Verderosa, in risposta all'agguato subito da "U' lepre", fu organizzato il tentato omicidio di Mimmo Falco, colpito in via della Repubblica, a Foggia, da colpi d’arma da fuoco a gola, petto e schiena che gli provocarono la paralisi permanente dell’attività di locomozione.

descriptions: Sinesi-Francavilla attacks Moretti-Pellegrino-Lanza affiliate Albanese, leading to further violence.
edges.evidence_quote: Il tentativo di omicidio di Sinesi scatena la vendetta del clan il 29 ottobre. Nel bar H24 di via san Severo, entrano due sicari che sparano all'indirizzo di tre persone tra cui proprio Giuseppe Albanese, che però scappa e riesce a chiudersi in bagno. Viene ucciso Roberto Tizzano e ferito Roberto Bruno, nipote di Vito Bruno Lanza.

descriptions: The two batteries are historically opposed but cooperate in racketeering through a common fund.
edges.evidence_quote: Viene documentata la contrapposizione tra le due “batterie” mafiose egemoni sul territorio (da una parte quella dei Sinesi-Francavilla e dall’altra quella dei Moretti-Pellegrino-Lanza) che sono comunque inquadrabili in un unico contesto all’interno della Società Foggiana . I due gruppi criminali storicamente in contrapposizione infatti sul fronte del racket chiudevano affari in sinergia e si spartivano i ricavi grazie ad una “cassa comune”.

descriptions: Historically warring clans federated to control cocaine trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Dalle indagini è emerso che alcuni clan storicamente in lotta tra di loro si erano federati per esercitare tutti insieme la pressione mafiosa in città nel traffico della cocaina.

descriptions: Rivalry between Sinesi-Francavilla and Trisciuoglio-Prencipe groups led to the fourth mafia war.
edges.evidence_quote: Stando alla ricostruzione della polizia e della Direzione Investigativa Antimafia, furono le rivalità tra il gruppo di Roberto Sinesi e quello di Trisciuoglio-Prencipe a portare alla quarta guerra di mafia

descriptions: The three clans have both conflicts and cooperative agreements for drug trafficking and extortion.
edges.evidence_quote: Nonostante queste tre batterie siano state spesso in conflitto, non sono mancati accordi con i quali sono stati costituiti assetti multipartecipativi ad esempio del traffico di droga o delle estorsioni per condividere e spartire il profitti.

descriptions: The war ended with an alliance between Sinesi-Francavilla and Trisciuoglio-Prencipe to manage the funeral business together.
edges.evidence_quote: La guerra si concluse con un'alleanza tra i due gruppi consolidata al fine di gestire insieme il ricco business dei funerali.

descriptions: Li Bergolis are allies of the Sinesi-Francavilla battery.
edges.evidence_quote: I clan predominanti sono i Li Bergolis di Monte Sant'Angelo (alleati della batteria foggiana dei Sinesi-Francavilla)

descriptions: Clan Sinesi-Francavilla is a component battery of Società foggiana.
edges.evidence_quote: La Società foggiana è priva di un vertice aggregante, ma è caratterizzata da una struttura interna compatta con organizzazione in 'batterie' su un modello federale a forte connotazione familiare.

edges.target: Union for the Rehabilitation of Democracy in Congo
descriptions: URDC is allied with M23.
edges.evidence_quote: Led by Tahanga Nyolo and allied with M23

descriptions: The Errol Flynns are the precursors to the Jeffries Boys, meaning the Jeffries Boys succeeded them.
edges.evidence_quote: The Errol Flynns are recognized as the precursors to most, if not all, Detroit gangs that followed in their wake. Some of the most notable successors include the "Be Like Boys", "Dexter Boys" (an offshoot of YBI), "Schoolcraft Boys or SCB's", "SNS", "Fenkell Boys", "FMK" ("Fenkell Mafia Killers"), "7 Mile Killers or 7 Mile Dogs", "Linwood Boys", "Brewster Boys", "Jeffries Boys", and "8 Mile sconys", ("JBFL") "Joy Boys" or "Exit 9"

edges.target: Servicios Especiales De Vigilancia y Seguridad Privada
descriptions: CONVIVIR groups merged into the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Many of the CONVIVIR groups simply joined up with the Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC).

descriptions: Convivir listed as ally of Bloque Metro in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Convivir (cooperativas de vigilancia)

edges.target: Servicios Especiales De Vigilancia y Seguridad Privada
descriptions: Bloque Metro allied with Convivir.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Convivir (cooperativas de vigilancia)

descriptions: ANM controlled Heroes of Return.
edges.evidence_quote: the ANM established the Abtal al-Audah (Heroes of Return) commando group in early summer 1966. The ANM exercised de facto control, unbeknownst to the PLO, which funded the outfit.

descriptions: ANM collaborated with PLA to create Heroes of Return.
edges.evidence_quote: In collaboration with the Palestine Liberation Organisation's (PLO) Palestinian Liberation Army (PLA), the ANM established the Abtal al-Audah (Heroes of Return) commando group in early summer 1966.

descriptions: PAC was established by ANM as its military wing.
edges.evidence_quote: In late 1963 the ANM leadership established the Palestinian Action Command (PAC), an internal military organisation with nominal authority over the ANM's Palestinian members.

edges.source: Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine
descriptions: PFLP originated from the ANM.
edges.evidence_quote: The PFLP grew out of the Harakat al-Qawmiyyin al-Arab, or Arab Nationalist Movement (ANM), founded in 1953 by George Habash.

Boricua Popular Army.description: The Boricua Popular Army, also known as Los Macheteros, is a clandestine militant organization based in Puerto Rico that campaigns for Puerto Rican independence from the United States. Founded in the 1970s, it was led by Filiberto Ojeda Ríos until his death in 2005. The group has claimed responsibility for bombings, attacks on US military personnel, and a 1983 Wells Fargo robbery. Since 2005, it has operated as an activist group.

descriptions: The EPB worked in conjunction with FALN.
edges.evidence_quote: In September 1979, the EPB revealed that it was working in conjunction with the Volunteers, FARP and FALN.

descriptions: The EPB worked in conjunction with FARP.
edges.evidence_quote: In September 1979, the EPB revealed that it was working in conjunction with the Volunteers, FARP and FALN.

descriptions: The EPB joined the FARP and the Volunteers in planting bombs.
edges.evidence_quote: On October 28, 1986, they joined the FARP and the Volunteers in planting eight bombs in militarily sites across Puerto Rico.

descriptions: The EPB worked in conjunction with the Volunteers.
edges.evidence_quote: In September 1979, the EPB revealed that it was working in conjunction with the Volunteers, FARP and FALN.

descriptions: The EPB joined the FARP and the Volunteers in planting bombs.
edges.evidence_quote: On October 28, 1986, they joined the FARP and the Volunteers in planting eight bombs in militarily sites across Puerto Rico.

descriptions: Boricua Popular Army is listed as an ally of Ñetas in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Boricua Popular Army, Martinez Familia Sangeros, La ONU

descriptions: Ñetas are allied with Ejército Popular Boricua.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Ejército Popular Boricua (sin confirmar)

descriptions: Jie Ji was created by an associate of Wah Kee's founder, indicating a structural tie.
edges.evidence_quote: Zhāng Xióngjié (张雄杰) created Jié Jì (杰记) in Vietnam

descriptions: Outback Blacks ambushed Mongrel Mob Kawerau chapter, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: On 13 January 2018, a gunfight erupted in Whakatāne, Bay of Plenty, when a funeral procession of the Mongrel Mob Kawerau chapter was ambushed by members of the Outback Blacks – a sub-chapter of Black Power.

descriptions: Grande Aracri and Russelli were allies in a faida against Arena, Trapasso, Dragone, and Megna.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 3 marzo 2000 ... scoppia una faida tra i Grande Aracri- Nicoscia -Capicchiano e Russelli e gli Arena - Trapasso - Dragone e Megna

descriptions: The Nicoscia are allied with the Russelli.
edges.evidence_quote: alleata con i Grande Aracri e i Russelli

descriptions: After MPCP's formal establishment, the paramilitary wing claimed the name Revolutionary Armed Forces of Peru.
edges.evidence_quote: after which it has claimed the name of Revolutionary Armed Forces of Peru ( Spanish : Fuerzas armadas revolucionarias del Perú ).

descriptions: Gallico is allied with Sciglitano.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono in alleanza con i Morgante, gli Sgrò e gli Sciglitano

descriptions: Forgione supported Alvaro in the faida di Sinopoli.
edges.evidence_quote: Gli Alvaro, appoggiati dalle famiglie Forgione e Violi, per vent'anni dovettero regolare i conti con i Filleti-De Angelis-Orfeo.

descriptions: Goda-ikka's founder was a member of Kagotora-gumi before forming his own group.
edges.evidence_quote: The group was formed in 1948 as the Goda-gumi ( 合田組 , Gōda-gumi ) in Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi by Koichi Goda, then a member of an old yakuza clan, the Kagotora-gumi.

descriptions: The Red Brigades killed two members of the MSI in a raid.
edges.evidence_quote: In June 1974, the Red Brigades killed two members of the Italian neo-fascist party, Movimento Sociale Italiano (MSI), during a raid on the MSI headquarters in Padua.

Lo Russo clan.description: The Lo Russo clan was a Neapolitan Camorra clan based in Miano, Naples, founded in the late 1970s by the Lo Russo brothers. It controlled numerous neighborhoods in northern Naples for over three decades, engaging in drug trafficking, extortion, and prostitution. The clan was part of the Nuova Famiglia and later the Secondigliano Alliance. Following arrests of its leaders, the organization is considered overthrown.

descriptions: The Lo Russo clan is a subgroup of the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: The Camorra's organizational structure is divided into individual groups called 'clans'.

descriptions: Lo Russo clan is a Camorra clan.
edges.evidence_quote: assets worth €20 million belonging to the Potenza brothers, who are considered to be linked to the Lo Russo clan, were seized in Lugano

descriptions: Lo Russo clan is allied with Birra-Iacomino and carried out a murder on their behalf.
edges.evidence_quote: Il compito fu affidato al clan alleato dei "Capitoni", ovvero i Lo Russo, in particolare alla squadra del reggente Raffaele Perfetto.

descriptions: Members of Lo Russo and Birra-Iacomino were arrested together for planning an ambush, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: nove persone, quattro del clan Lo Russo di Miano e cinque dei Birra-Iacomino, vennero arrestate in quanto ritenute coinvolte nella pianificazione e nell'esecuzione dell'imboscata di Mario Ascione

descriptions: A split from Misso was led by a man linked to the Lo Russo clan, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2005, approfittando dell'assenza di 'o nasone scoppia un'altra sanguinosa faida nella Sanità, che ha visto come protagonista di una scissione il gruppo facente capo ad un uomo legato al clan Lo Russo: Salvatore Torino detto 'o gassusaro'.

descriptions: The Giuliano clan fought wars against the Lo Russo clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Le numerose guerre tra gli anni ottanta e novanta contro i Contini , i Licciardi , i Lo Russo , i Misso e i Mazzarella depotenziano però notevolmente il clan Giuliano

descriptions: Infobox lists Lo Russo clan as a rival of Licciardi clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Lo Russo clan (defunct)

descriptions: Lo Russo clan acted as intermediary between Di Lauro clan and secessionists.
edges.evidence_quote: During the Scampia feud, the Lo Russo clan had a role as an intermediary between the Di Lauro clan and the so-called 'secessionists'

descriptions: Esposito-Genidoni clan is listed as a rival of Lo Russo clan in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Licciardi clan, Esposito-Genidoni clan

descriptions: Licciardi clan is listed as a rival of Lo Russo clan in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Licciardi clan, Esposito-Genidoni clan

descriptions: Lo Russo clan fought against the NCO as part of the NF.
edges.evidence_quote: The war against the NCO resulted in the victory of the Lo Russo's and other NF clans.

descriptions: Lo Russo clan merged into the Nuova Famiglia alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: After its formation, it immediately merged into the Nuova Famiglia (NF), a federation of Anti-Cutolo Camorra clans

descriptions: Piccirillo clan is listed as an ally of Lo Russo clan in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Scissionisti di Secondigliano, Vastarella clan, Piccirillo clan, and formerly Secondigliano Alliance

edges.evidence_quote: During the Scampia feud, the Lo Russo clan had a role as an intermediary between the Di Lauro clan and the so-called 'secessionists'

edges.evidence_quote: After the end of the NF alliance, the Lo Russo clan joined the newly formed Secondigliano Alliance

descriptions: Lo Russo clan was in conflict with the Stabile clan.
edges.evidence_quote: The Lo Russo clan, for its good organization, also had a leading role in a conflict with the Stabile

descriptions: Vastarella clan is listed as an ally of Lo Russo clan in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Scissionisti di Secondigliano, Vastarella clan, Piccirillo clan, and formerly Secondigliano Alliance

descriptions: Lo Russo clan is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lo Russo clan (defunct)

descriptions: After splitting, the Lo Russo clan fought a gang war against the Secondigliano Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The Lo Russo clan eventually split from the alliance, leading to a violent gang war against the other clans which claimed 120 lives in Naples and the surrounding region.

descriptions: Lo Russo clan is a member of the Secondigliano Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Apart from the Licciardi clan, the alliance included the Contini, Mallardo, Lo Russo, Stabile, Prestieri, Bocchetti and Di Lauro clans.

descriptions: Lo Russo clan was initially an ally of the Secondigliano Alliance, then split and became hostile.
edges.evidence_quote: The Lo Russo clan , then an important ally of the Alliance, disagreed and repackaged the shipment for sale on the streets, resulting in the deaths of many. ... The Lo Russo clan eventually split from the alliance, leading to a violent gang war against the other clans which claimed 120 lives in Naples and the surrounding region.

descriptions: Stabile and Lo Russo, formerly allies, fought a conflict in 2003-2004.
edges.evidence_quote: faida di Chiaiano : conflitto svoltosi nel corso del 2003 e 2004 a Chiaiano tra il clan Stabile e il clan Lo Russo, in precedenza alleati sotto la bandiera dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano

DeCavalcante crime family.description: The DeCavalcante crime family is an Italian American Mafia family operating primarily in North Jersey, with ties to New York's Five Families. Founded around the 1900s by Phil Amari, it engages in racketeering, gambling, drug trafficking, and other illicit activities. The family is sometimes called the 'Sixth Family' due to its influence and connections.

descriptions: The DeCavalcante family is a constituent family of the American Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Las familias criminales afectadas incluyen a las Cinco Familias de Nueva York así como la familia criminal DeCavalcante de Nueva Jersey

Asano-gumi.description: The Asano-gumi is a yakuza group based in Okayama, Japan. It was formed in 1945 as the Oyama-ikka and restarted as the Asano-gumi in 1952. The group has been involved in conflicts with the Yamaguchi-gumi and is a member of the anti-Yamaguchi federation Gosha-kai. It is a designated yakuza group with about 40 members.

descriptions: Asano-gumi is a member of the Gosha-kai federation since 1996.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 1996, the Asano-gumi has been a member of an anti-Yamaguchi federation named the Gosha-kai, along with three other Chugoku-based organizations, the Kyosei-kai, the Kyodo-kai, the Goda-ikka, and the Shikoku-based Shinwa-kai.

descriptions: Asano-gumi had conflicts with Kyodo-kai in the late 20th century.
edges.evidence_quote: The Asano-gumi caused conflicts with the Yamaguchi-gumi and the Kyodo-kai in the late 20th century.

descriptions: Asano-gumi and Yamaguchi-gumi were in conflict, with violent incidents including murders in 1987.
edges.evidence_quote: The Asano-gumi caused conflicts with the Yamaguchi-gumi and the Kyodo-kai in the late 20th century. In one notable case, Asano member(s) shot and murdered two Yamaguchi members in Kurashiki in 1987, as a retaliation for an earlier attack by Yamaguchi member(s) involving carving knives against two Asano members.

descriptions: Goda-ikka and Asano-gumi are both members of the Gosha-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 1996, the Goda-ikka has been a member of an anti- Yamaguchi federation, the Gosha-kai, along with three other Chugoku -based organizations, the Kyosei-kai , the Kyodo-kai , the Asano-gumi , and the Shikoku -based Shinwa-kai .

descriptions: Kyodo-kai and Asano-gumi are both members of the Gosha-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 1996, the Kyodo-kai has been a member of an anti-Yamaguchi federation named the Gosha-kai, along with three other Chugoku-based organizations, the Kyosei-kai, the Asano-gumi, the Goda-ikka, and the Shikoku-based Shinwa-kai.

descriptions: Kyosei-kai formed Gosha-kai with Asano-gumi as a fellow member.
edges.evidence_quote: and has formed a new anti-Yamaguchi federation named the Gosha-kai since 1996 with three other Chugoku-based organizations, the Kyodo-kai, the Asano-gumi, the Goda-ikka, and the Shikoku-based Shinwa-kai.

descriptions: Shinwa-kai and Asano-gumi are both members of the Gosha-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 1996, the Shinwa-kai has been a member of an anti- Yamaguchi federation named the Gosha-kai, along with four Chugoku -based organizations, the Kyosei-kai , the Kyodo-kai , the Goda-ikka , and the Asano-gumi .
